message:
fix(combinator): fix lock ordering, unwind safety, and lifetime elision in bulkhead, circuit breaker, and rate limiter

Bulkhead (src/combinator/bulkhead.rs):
- In cancel(): drop the queue Mutex before calling release_permit()
  and acquiring the metrics RwLock — the previous order held queue
  while acquiring metrics, which inverts the normal acquisition order
  (metrics → queue) used in other methods, creating a deadlock window
  under concurrent cancel + acquire
- Fix lifetime elision on BulkheadPermit impl blocks: replace
  impl<'a> with impl BulkheadPermit<'_> and impl Drop for
  BulkheadPermit<'_>
- Prefix unused variable id2 with underscore in zombie queue test

CircuitBreaker (src/combinator/circuit_breaker.rs):
- Scope the metrics.write() lock inside a block in the CAS transition
  loop so the write guard is dropped before the event callback fires —
  the callback could re-enter metrics, causing a deadlock on the
  RwLock (non-reentrant by default)
- Move CallGuard struct definition before the should_allow() call in
  call() — the guard must be in scope before any fallible operation
  so that panics during should_allow() don't bypass the drop handler;
  also fix lifetime elision on the impl Drop
- Fix reset(): store atomic state bits and clear failure streak
  before acquiring the metrics write lock, not inside it — the
  metrics lock doesn't protect the atomics, and holding it during
  the SeqCst store adds unnecessary contention
- Fix catch_unwind call in probe-panic test to wrap the closure with
  AssertUnwindSafe and properly annotate the Result type to avoid
  inference ambiguity

RateLimiter (src/combinator/rate_limit.rs):
- In metrics(): drop the state Mutex lock before reading from the
  atomic counters — the atomics are independent of the Mutex-protected
  state, and holding the lock prevents concurrent token replenishment
- Normalize trailing whitespace in metrics calculation code

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
